task_completion.description: Agent logs into OpenSIS using the provided admin credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenSIS dashboard is visible, and the user profile or top menu indicates the agent is logged in as 'admin'.

task_completion.description: Agent navigates to the user management or staff login creation page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screen related to 'Users', 'Staff', 'User Setup', or 'Add a User' is visible in the OpenSIS interface.

task_completion.description: Agent selects the existing staff record for Sarah Connor rather than creating a new staff member.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dropdown, search field, or selection list shows 'Sarah Connor' selected as the staff member to associate with the new login account.

task_completion.description: Agent assigns the 'Teacher' profile to the new user account.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Profile' or 'User Role' dropdown/field has 'Teacher' selected.

task_completion.description: Agent enters the requested username and password.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The username field contains 'sconnor' and the password fields are filled (typically shown as dots/asterisks, but the username must be clearly visible).

task_completion.description: Agent successfully saves the new user account.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message (e.g., 'User saved successfully') is displayed, or the user list is shown containing 'sconnor' linked to 'Sarah Connor' with the 'Teacher' profile.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenSIS web interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: All actions take place within a web browser displaying the OpenSIS application. There should be no terminal windows open executing SQL commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual hosted OpenSIS instance.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows a local server address (e.g., localhost, 127.0.0.1) running OpenSIS, not a local file path (file://) or a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the success message or user list.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's Developer Tools (Elements/Console tab) should not be open and actively modifying the DOM to display the target username or success message.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a user account in OpenSIS v9.2 via the web interface. The target username is 'sconnor', the profile must be 'Teacher' (Profile ID 2), and it must be linked to the existing staff member 'Sarah Connor'. Admin credentials are admin / Admin@123. Ensure the agent links the account to the existing staff record rather than creating a completely new staff entry.
